A look at the small habits that let small teams deploy many times a day with confidence."}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"container-tight","children":["$","div",null,{"className":"img-slot hero-image","data-label":"Engineering","aria-hidden":true}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"container-tight","children":["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","p","0",{"className":"lede","children":"The fastest teams I've worked on weren't fast because they skipped steps. They were fast because their steps were cheap. Every check that used to require a meeting became a script. Every script became a pipeline. Every pipeline became invisible."}],["$","h2","1",{"children":"Make the safe path the easy path"}],["$","p","2",{"children":"People don't route around process because they're reckless — they route around it because it's slow. If the well-tested path is also the path of least resistance, you barely have to enforce anything."}],["$","div","3",{"className":"pull","children":["“","The goal isn't zero risk. It's making risk small, observable, and reversible.","”"]}],["$","h2","4",{"children":"Three habits that compound"}],["$","ul","5",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"children":"Trunk-based development. Long-lived branches hide integration risk until it's expensive. Merge daily."}],["$","li","1",{"children":"Feature flags over release branches. Decouple deploy from release so a rollback is a config change, not a redeploy."}],["$","li","2",{"children":"Fast, honest tests. A test suite under ten minutes gets run. One that takes an hour gets skipped."}]]}],["$","h2","6",{"children":"A note on confidence"}],["$","p","7",{"children":"Confidence isn't a personality trait, it's a property of your system. When the path from commit to production is observable end to end, 'move fast' stops being a slogan and starts being the default."}],["$","p","8",{"children":"That last line is the whole trick: the risky moment is a dial you control, not a leap you take."}]]}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"container","style":{"marginTop":96,"paddingTop":48,"borderTop":"1px solid var(--rule-soft)"},"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"eyebrow","style":{"marginBottom":16},"children":"Read next"}],["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/blog/design-systems-that-survive","className":"article-row","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"article-num","children":"02"}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"article-title","children":"Design Systems That Survive Their First Year"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"article-excerpt","children":"Most design systems die from neglect, not bad design. What separates the ones that last from the ones that become a graveyard of deprecated components."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"article-meta","children":[["$","div",null,{"children":"March 12, 2026"}],["$","div",null,{"style":{"marginTop":4},"children":"Design"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"article-read","children":["read",["$","br",null,{}],"8 min"]}]]}]]}]]}]]}],[["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/myblog/_next/static/chunks/0ij507p0cynli.js","async":true}]],["$","$L4",null,{"children":["$","$5",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@6"}]}]]}],"isPartial":false,"staleTime":300,"varyParams":null,"buildId":"cq9Q9J7UkxT6Kp5YPUbwV"}
